shots.speech_text: Imagine 26.8%. That single number is the key to understanding Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments — one of the most extraordinary phenomena in the known universe.

shots.speech_text: Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments sits within the domain of physics_cosmology. Galaxy rotation curves (Vera Rubin, 1970s) show stars orbiting at constant velocity far beyond the visible disk — requiring total enclosed mass to grow linearly with radius, implying an invisible extended 'dark matter halo' vastly more massive than the luminous galaxy. To understand it, we need to think at a scale that challenges intuition.
shots.visual_direction: ESTABLISH SHOT: [Wide or macro view establishing scale and context of Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments]. Slow zoom or pull-back revealing scope. Minimal text — let the imagery speak.
shots.vo_notes: HELIX voiceover — warm measured female voice, slight British RP accent, inviting and professional — a trusted guide through the unknown, never clinical, wonder without hyperbole
shots.role_description: Set the scene: what is this phenomenon, where does it occur, what scale are we at

shots.speech_text: Galaxy rotation curves (Vera Rubin, 1970s) show stars orbiting at constant velocity far beyond the visible disk — requiring total enclosed mass to grow linearly with radius, implying an invisible extended 'dark matter halo' vastly more massive than the luminous galaxy The Bullet Cluster (1E 0657-56) provides direct empirical separation of dark matter from ordinary matter: during the collision of two galaxy clusters, the hot X-ray-emitting gas (ordinary matter, ~85% of baryons) was electromagnetically slowed and remained between the clusters, while the gravitational lensing mass map shows two dark matter concentrations that passed through each other collisionlessly and now coincide with the galaxies — offset at 8σ significance The governing framework here is Lambda-CDM (standard cosmological model); Gravitational lensing (weak and strong); Galactic dynamics; Particle physics (WIMP, axion, sterile neutrino candidates).
shots.visual_direction: EXPLAIN SEQUENCE: [Animation or diagram illustrating the core mechanism of Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments]. Clear, elegant — no clutter. Labels only where essential. Motion should mirror the narration pace.

shots.speech_text: What you're seeing here — The Bullet Cluster: one of the most visually striking composite astronomical images — combines three datasets. Blue false-color regions show the gravitational lensing mass map (the.
shots.visual_direction: VISUALIZATION: [The Bullet Cluster: one of the most visually striking composite astronomical images — combines three datasets. Blue false-color regions show the gravitational lensing mass map (the dark matter); pink/magenta regions show the Chandra X-ray emission (ordinary hot gas); visible-light image of galaxies. The galaxies and blue dark matter halos are on the left and right (having passed through each other), while the pink gas is squeezed between them in the middle — a vivid visual proof of dark matter's existence. The bow shock on the leading edge of the 'bullet' subcluster is a dramatic arc of heated gas. Vera Rubin visualization: overlaid plots of expected rotation curve (falling off like Keplerian, dashed line) vs. observed rotation curve (flat, solid line) for 21 spiral galaxies — the gap between expected and observed is the dark matter signature. Scale: the dark matter halo of the Milky Way extends to ~200 kpc (650,000 light-years), roughly 5x the visible disk diameter. Shot ideas: animated Bullet Cluster collision with separating color layers; split-panel rotation curve graph (expected vs observed for Andromeda); underground dark matter detector visual (xenon tank glowing blue in a cavern).]. HELIX narrates over continuous visual — no cuts interrupting the flow.

shots.role_description: Describe what the camera or animation shows — specific, visual, sensory language

shots.speech_text: The numbers confirm it. Bullet Cluster dark matter offset from X-ray gas detected at 8σ significance — the gravitational lensing mass concentrations coincide with the galaxies, not the gas, providing direct empirical evidence for collisionless dark matter. This result — published by Clowe et al. 2006, ApJL 648 L109 ('A Direct Empirical Proof of the Existence of Dark Matter') — represents one of the landmark measurements in modern physics_cosmology.

shots.role_description: The key measurement, landmark discovery, or institutional proof — cite it precisely

shots.speech_text: Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments is not just a phenomenon at the far edge of science. It connects directly to questions about the fabric of reality. The The Bullet Cluster (1E 0657-56) provides direct empirical separation of dark matter from ordinary matter: during the collision of two galaxy clusters, the hot X-ray-emitting gas (ordinary matter, ~85% of baryons) was electromagnetically slowed and remained between the clusters, while the gravitational lensing mass map shows two dark matter concentrations that passed through each other collisionlessly and now coincide with the galaxies — offset at 8σ significance has implications that reach far beyond the laboratory.

shots.speech_text: We are only beginning to understand Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments. And perhaps that's exactly the point — the universe keeps its most intricate secrets just within reach, if we keep looking.

provenance_card.title: Dark Matter — Observational Evidence and Current Detection Experiments
provenance_card.key_measurement: Dark matter constitutes 26.8% of total mass-energy content of the universe (and 85% of total matter); Bullet Cluster dark matter offset detected at 8σ statistical significance; Galaxy rotation curves flat to 200+ kpc in spiral galaxies vs. Keplerian falloff predicted for visible matter alone (Planck Collaboration 2020, A&A 641 A6; Clowe et al. 2006, ApJL 648 L109)
provenance_card.framework: Lambda-CDM (standard cosmological model); Gravitational lensing (weak and strong); Galactic dynamics; Particle physics (WIMP, axion, sterile neutrino candidates)
institutions: Chandra X-ray Center / Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory
Carnegie Institution for Science — Vera Rubin's original rotation curve work
LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) Collaboration — Sanford Underground Research Facility, South Dakota
XENON Collaboration — Gran Sasso National Laboratory, Italy
PandaX Collaboration — China Jinping Underground Laboratory

sources.title: Bullet Cluster dark matter offset from X-ray gas detected at 8σ significance — the gravitational lensing mass concentrations coincide with the galaxies, not the gas, providing direct empirical evidence for collisionless dark matter
sources.citation: Clowe et al. 2006, ApJL 648 L109 ('A Direct Empirical Proof of the Existence of Dark Matter')

sources.title: Vera Rubin and Kent Ford (1970) measured rotation curves of spiral galaxies showing flat velocity profiles to large radii, implying dark matter halos extending far beyond visible stars
sources.citation: Rubin & Ford 1970, ApJ 159 379 (Andromeda rotation curve); Rubin, Ford & Thonnard 1978, ApJL 225 L107 (extended galaxy sample)

sources.title: Planck 2018 CMB results: dark matter density Ω_c h² = 0.1200 ± 0.0012, corresponding to 26.8% of total energy density; ordinary (baryonic) matter is 4.9%; dark energy 68.3%
sources.citation: Planck Collaboration 2020, A&A 641 A6 (Planck 2018 Results VI: Cosmological Parameters)

sources.title: Bullet Cluster X-ray observations by Chandra show 70 million K gas in subcluster plowing through 100 million K main cluster gas at ~4,500 km/s; merger occurred ~150 million years ago from Earth's perspective
sources.citation: Markevitch et al. 2004, ApJ 606 819 (Chandra constraints on dark matter self-interaction cross-section from the Bullet Cluster)

sources.title: LUX-ZEPLIN (LZ) experiment reached world-leading WIMP sensitivity in 2022 with no confirmed detections, setting spin-independent WIMP-nucleon cross-section upper limits of ~6.5 × 10⁻⁴⁸ cm² at 30 GeV/c² WIMP mass

provenance_card.accuracy_note: All measurements cited. Uncertainty stated where present. No fabricated statistics.

guardrails: HELIX is voiceover only — no on-screen host, no synthetic presenter, no avatar.
All quantitative claims must include units and citation.
Uncertainty must be stated where present (e.g. 'approximately', 'within a margin of').
Do not present theoretical models as confirmed fact without qualification.
Do not fabricate measurements, paper titles, DOIs, or institutional affiliations.
Maintain wonder without hyperbole — the phenomena are extraordinary enough.
No speculation presented as established science.
HELIX does not cover history, biography, or language — those are DAVID's domain.
